t_ch: 家人，想开点吧，都是为了知更鸟小姐。
t_en: Fam, keep an open mind. This is all for Miss Robin.

t_ch: 唉。
t_en: *sigh*

t_ch: 唉。
t_en: *sigh*

t_ch: 唉。
t_en: *sigh*

t_ch: 为热爱买单，不丢人。
t_en: There's no shame in paying for your passion.

t_ch: 勇敢的人先享受世界，别等热情耗尽了才后悔。信用点都准备好了吗，这就……
t_en: The bold get first dibs on everything. Don't wait until your passion wanes and regret sets in. Have you got all your credits ready, then? Here you go...

t_ch: 不知何时，你发现身后忽然多出了几个高大的身影。
t_en: You're not sure when, but at some point, you notice several tall figures suddenly standing behind you.

t_ch: 咳咳，作为唱片公司的合作仓库经理，利用职务便利囤积居奇，扰乱市场，非法销售，总算逮到现行了。
t_en: Ahem, misusing your position as the collaborating warehouse manager of the record company to stockpile albums, manipulate markets, and conduct illegal sales... You've been caught red-handed.

t_ch: 还有你们几个，明知是非法渠道仍旧购买，助长犯罪气焰，是不是也该处罚？
t_en: And you lot: You all knew it was illegal, but still aided and abetted a criminal. Should I punish you, too?

t_ch: 我们也是受害者啊。
t_en: We, too, are victims here.

t_ch: …这不是在钓鱼嘛。
t_en: ...Are you trying to bait us?

t_ch: 暂且放过你们，等把这个倒卖团伙全部逮捕后再清算。别跑，给我站住——！
t_en: I'll let you be for now and we'll settle things once the scalping syndicate members have all been locked up. Freeze. Don't run...!

t_ch: 倒卖专辑的逐梦客见势不妙，直接跑得不见人影了。猎犬在身后穷追不舍。
t_en: Realizing his time is up, the Dreamchaser scalper flees the scene without a trace. The Bloodhound agent gives chase.

t_ch: 这下好了。知更鸟小姐的新专辑是彻底买不到了。
t_en: That's it, then. There's absolutely no way to score Miss Robin's new album.

t_ch: 可恨的猎犬，偏偏这个时候冲出来…等我们拿到货再抓也不迟啊！
t_en: Those darn Bloodhounds would pick a time like this to make a raid... They can just wait until we've grabbed the album and collar us then!

t_ch: 咦，你们看…那个倒卖贩的包落在地面上……
t_en: Tsk, check it out... That scalper dropped his bag on the ground...

t_ch: 里面装的该不会是……
t_en: Don't tell me that we're going to find inside...

t_ch: 你心里危险的预感是对的，包里恰好装着三张知更鸟的新专辑。
t_en: Your gut feeling is right, and there are three copies of Miss Robin's new album inside the bag.

t_ch: 喔！
t_en: Whoa!

t_ch: 喔！
t_en: Whoa!

t_ch: 我们…是三个人？
t_en: There are... three of us?

t_ch: 一切都刚刚好。
t_en: Everything worked out just fine in the end.

t_ch: 现在，考验你的时候终于到了。你可以偷偷带走属于你的那张专辑，假装什么都没有发生过，也可以将捡来的物品交还到它本应在的地方。
t_en: Now, it's time to test your mettle. You can sneak off with your album and pretend that nothing happened — or, you can return the missing item back where it belongs.

t_ch: 你的选择是——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: …偷偷带走。
t_en: ...to sneak off with it.

t_ch: 好吧，谁叫你是知更鸟的忠实歌迷呢。还是那句话——为热爱做出牺牲，不丢人。于是你果断采取了行动……
t_en: Alright. You sure are a big fan of Miss Robin. Just like before — there's no shame in making a sacrifice for your passion. And so, you take your decisive action...

t_ch: 咳咳。我们还没走远呢。
t_en: Ahem. We haven't left yet.

t_ch: 身后传来熟悉的声音，你意识到自己的违法行为再次被抓住了。
t_en: A familiar voice comes from behind you. You realize that you've been busted once again for breaking the law.

t_ch: ——逮捕！打算干什么？手脚不干净啊，知更鸟小姐的粉丝就这素质？
t_en: ...You're under arrest! What are you gonna do about it? Caught once more with your hand in the cookie jar — is this the caliber of Miss Robin's fans?

t_ch: 算了，看你们也是诚心热爱。实在想要的话，给你们也不是不行，但得按市场价付钱，我们会转交给鸢尾花家系，就当是讨回货款了。
t_en: Whatever, I can tell that you're truly passionate about her. If you really want it, I suppose I could let you have it — but you'll have to pay the market price. We'll pass on the funds to the Iris Family and consider the bill paid.

t_ch: 就问这一次——要买么？
t_en: I'll only ask this once: Would you like to buy it?

t_ch: 看来你别无选择，并且还不得不多付出点代价来抚慰你那支离破碎的道德感。
t_en: Looks like you have no choice but to stump up a little cash to soothe your shattered sense of decency.

t_ch: 还给猎犬家系。
t_en: Give it back to the Bloodhound Family.

t_ch: 对，这才像话，你向来都是遵纪守法的好公民，即使偶尔误入歧途，也不会再犯错。只是…该去哪找刚才的那几个猎犬家系成员呢？
t_en: Yes, now that's more like it. You've always been a good law-abiding citizen even if you're sometimes misled, and you won't be making any more mistakes. If only... if only you could find those Bloodhound Family members from just before.

t_ch: 哼，还是被那家伙逃掉了。
t_en: Hmph, we lost him.

t_ch: 身后传来熟悉的声音，你赶忙将地面的袋子交给了他们。
t_en: A familiar voice shouts out from behind. You hurry to turn over the lost bag to them.

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 家人，不是吧？
t_en: What? No way!

t_ch: 谢谢你啊！要是连货都没带回去，猎犬的脸面就真丢光了。
t_en: Thank you! We'd have utterly shamed the Bloodhound lineage if we went back empty-handed.

t_ch: 看来你的善行得到了回报。
t_en: Looks like your good deed has paid off.

t_ch: 对了，你是知更鸟小姐的粉丝吧？这张新专辑就送给你做奖励好了。我们去和鸢尾花家系说明，相信他们不会吝啬这点资产。
t_en: That's right — you're a fan of Miss Robin, aren't you? Take this album of hers as a little reward. We'll explain everything to the Iris Family. I'm sure they won't be tight-fisted about this.

t_ch: 听说附近有高价倒卖《空气蛹》的逐梦客，你知道在哪吗？
t_en: I heard there's a Dreamchaser around here scalping the INSIDE album. Do you know where?

t_ch: 长官，走这边！
t_en: Right this way, captain!

t_ch: 好，我去多叫几个人。你继续陪那个逐梦客耍耍，别让他溜了。
t_en: Great. I'll call for more back up. You keep stringing that Dreamchaser along, and don't let him sneak off.

t_ch: 敢损害知更鸟小姐名声的人，绝对不能放过！
t_en: Whosoever dares sully the name of Miss Robin, we shall brook them no quarter!

t_ch: …朋友，你回来了？是不是到处逛了一圈后发现，我这里才是最低价？
t_en: ...Hey buddy, you're back? Did you ask around and find out that my price really is the cheapest?

t_ch: 买吧，不会吃亏的。
t_en: Buy it. You're not getting a bad deal.

t_ch: 家人，要是连新专辑都舍不得买，是要被开除粉籍的。
t_en: Fam, you'll be booted from the fan club if you're not even willing to stump up and buy the new album.

t_ch: 你这是违法行为。
t_en: That's against the law.

t_ch: 走，跟我去自首！
t_en: Come with me and turn yourself in!

t_ch: 嘿，还真让你给装起来了。客气几句，就以为自己是个大人物了？
t_en: Ha, you're really hamming it up now. A couple of nice platitudes, and now you think you're a big shot, huh?

t_ch: 我既然敢做这门生意，自然是有备而来。再不识好歹的话，就别怪我下手重了！
t_en: Since I dare to do this business, then of course I come ready for anything. Don't blame me for being too harsh if you don't know what's good for you!

t_ch: 咳咳——不知何时，你发现身后忽然多出了几个高大的身影。
t_en: Ahem, you're not sure when, but at some point you notice several tall figures suddenly standing behind you.

t_ch: 要不要试试，到底是谁下手会更重？
t_en: Wanna try me on for size then, and find out who is really harsher?

t_ch: 作为唱片公司的合作仓库经理，利用职务便利囤积居奇，扰乱市场，非法销售，总算逮到现行了——逮捕！
t_en: Misusing your position as the collaborating warehouse manager of the record company to stockpile albums, manipulate markets, and conduct illegal sales... You've been caught red-handed, and you're under arrest!

t_ch: 你怎么还带人来的啊！！！
t_en: What are you doing bringing other people along!?

t_ch: …我是正义的银河球棒侠！
t_en: ...I am the Galactic Baseballer of justice!

t_ch: 任何邪恶…终将绳之以法！
t_en: Any and all evil... will be brought to justice!

t_ch: 啧，只收缴到一张知更鸟小姐的新专辑，这家伙到底卖出去了多少啊？
t_en: Tsk, we've only managed to recover one copy of Miss Robin's album. How many has this guy already scalped?

t_ch: …你们不会也买了吧？要是被这个人骗了钱，可以把专辑还回来，鸢尾花家系会照价补偿。
t_en: ...Did you guys buy copies, too? If you've been scammed, you can return the album, and the Iris Family will compensate you for it.

t_ch: 没有。我们只是路过…对，路过。
t_en: Nah. We were just, uh... passing by. Yeah, just passing by.

t_ch: 跟我们完全没关系。
t_en: Absolutely nothing to do with us.

t_ch: 行，愿意被骗是你们自己的事。至于你，这张新专辑就送给你做奖励好了。我们去和鸢尾花家系说明，相信他们也不会吝啬这点资产。
t_en: Alright. If you're happy being scammed, then that's on you. And as for you — take this new album as a little reward. We'll explain everything to the Iris Family. I'm sure they won't be tight-fisted about this tiny asset.

t_ch: 就到这吧。事情都办完了，我还得赶着去参加知更鸟小姐的粉丝聚会呢。
t_en: That's it, then — all sorted. I have to rush off and join up with the party at Miss Robin's fan gathering.

t_ch: 看啊！是知更鸟小姐！
t_en: Check it out — it's Miss Robin!

t_ch: 等这张新专辑好久了！
t_en: I've been holding out for this new album for ages!

t_ch: 不知道能不能抢到…
t_en: Hope I can grab one...

t_ch: 没预购的自觉退群！
t_en: If you haven't pre-ordered, please leave the group!

t_ch: 我先下单一百张！
t_en: I'm gonna buy a hundred!

t_ch: 求蹭个组团的车~
t_en: Can I hitch a ride with someone~?

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐放心飞！
t_en: Fly, Miss Robin — fly high!

t_ch: 我们永相随！
t_en: We will always be with you!

t_ch: 用行动表示支持！
t_en: Show her support with your actions!

t_ch: 知更鸟小姐！
t_en: Miss Robin!

t_ch: 我一直都是你的粉丝！
t_en: I've always been your fan!

t_ch: 这是我们家族的明星！
t_en: She's the star of our Family!

t_ch: 哎！你——对对对、就是你！你就是我们的新老板吧，没错吧没错吧！
t_en: Hey! You... Yeah, you! You must be our new boss! Am I right? I'm right, aren't I?

t_ch: 让我好好瞧瞧——嗯…不错、不错，看看这面相、这气质，准是个英明神武明察秋毫神机妙算武……
t_en: Let's have a look-see... Mm, not bad. This face, this poise, you must be a brilliantly deified avatar of divine erudition with godly perspicacity...

t_ch: 停！说人话…
t_en: Stop! Speak human, please...

t_ch: 说吧，想让我做什么？
t_en: Cut to the chase, what do you need me to do?

t_ch: 咳…总之就是，老板你让我一见如故，我总觉得得和你好好唠唠……
t_en: Ahem... What I meant is that you've got old friend vibes, and I feel like I simply have to chat you up...

t_ch: 现在不是工作时间吗？
t_en: Isn't it working hours right now?

t_ch: 拉着新老板摸鱼是吧。
t_en: You trying to get your new boss to slack off with you?

t_ch: 哎！这…冤枉、冤枉啊！我们自动乐团向来主打一个爱岗敬业、吃苦耐劳，怎么可能上班摸鱼呢？可真是天——大的误会！
t_en: Hey! I... It's not like that at all! The automated orchestra has always been loyally industrious and hardworking to a fault! We wouldn't slack off during work hours! This... This is truly a horrible misunderstanding!

t_ch: 我想和你唠的可都是正事！是为了提升咱们的工作效率、改善咱们的演出环境——
t_en: I wanted to chat to you about work stuff! About how to increase our work efficiency, to improve on our performance environment...

t_ch: 你瞧这台上台下…对，仔细瞧，有没有看出点什么来？
t_en: Look at this stage, on and off it... Yep, scrutinize it carefully: What do you see?

t_ch: 就见你消极怠工了。
t_en: All I see is someone not doing their job.

t_ch: 哎！都说了不是了…算了，我也不和你卖关子了。
t_en: Hey! I already told you it's not like that... Forget it. I'll just get straight to the point.

t_ch: 好像稍微有点冷清？
t_en: It seems a bit quiet, doesn't it?

t_ch: 哎！这就对喽，要不怎么说老板就是老板呢！
t_en: Yep! That's absolutely right! That's why you're the boss, eh?

t_ch: 我是觉得吧，这台上天天只有我们这帮自动乐器在演奏，曲子来来回回也就那么几首，观众们听多了也怪没劲的。
t_en: What I'm thinking, is that it's just us automated instruments performing day-in, day-out. We've only got a few tunes in our repertoire, and it's not the least bit surprising that the audience gets bored after repeated showings.

t_ch: 话说在前头，你可别说什么「怎么不学点新曲子」之类的啊，我们倒是想呢，可之前的老板总是要讲究个「品味格调」，就只让我们翻来覆去演奏那几个调子。
t_en: And I'll pre-empt you: Don't say something like "Why don't you learn new tunes" or something along these lines. We'd love that, but our previous boss was always preoccupied with "sense of style and taste," and would only allow us to play these few pieces over and over.

t_ch: 我看老板你是个英明的开明的精明的…咳、总之是个明白人，所以就想着给出点合理建议——
t_en: I reckoned that in all your brilliance and enlightenment and astuteness... Ahem, you know what's up anyway, so I thought I'd offer a suggestion...

t_ch: 要是能请几个明星歌手来热热场、撑撑门面，这不就热闹起来了吗。
t_en: If we invite a few celebrity singers to warm the crowd up and hype up our show, that'd get them all enthused and lively, wouldn't it?

t_ch: 你想让我请谁？
t_en: Who do you want me to invite?

t_ch: 请知更鸟怎么样…
t_en: How about Miss Robin...

t_ch: 嘶——算了算了，就咱这小场子…老板你要不再想想别的吧。
t_en: Psh... Forget it, such a pitiful stage like ours... How about someone more attainable?

t_ch: 其实咱的要求也不高，有没有名气不重要，唱的好就成…对了，我还有个小小的不情之请……
t_en: It's not like our expectations are all that high. Fame is secondary, as long as the person sings well... Oh yes, I also have a teensy-weensy favor...

t_ch: 就是…呃…老板你能带点乐谱回来吗？之前那些曲子我们早就腻歪得不行，就连观众们也都听出茧子来了……
t_en: It's... er... Boss, can you bring some music scores back? We've tired of those pieces since a long time ago, and even the crowd's spinning cobwebs just listening to the same tunes...

t_ch: 也不是不行…
t_en: Sounds doable...

t_ch: 这明明就是你的分内事！
t_en: This is supposed to be your job!

t_ch: 我知道，找新曲、排新歌都是我的分内事。但咱和空艇签了约，平时吃住都在艇上，咱可不想违约…所以才斗胆求到老板你这里……
t_en: I know, I know, finding and composing new music are supposed to be part of my duty. But we've signed a contract with the airship, and we practically live on it. I'm afraid to violate the terms... which is why I'm sorta begging you here...

t_ch: 哎！老板英明——那就这么说好了啊，等你好消息！
t_en: Hey! You're the best, boss! It's settled then. I'll wait for your good news!

t_ch: 总这么冷冷清清 …
t_en: It's always this quiet...

t_ch: 早晚得得忧郁症…
t_en: I'll get depression sooner or later...

t_ch: 怎么样怎么样，想好人选了吗、乐谱找来了吗？
t_en: How'd it go!? You have a person in mind? And you found any music scores?

t_ch: 我认识一位歌手，叫柯柯娜…
t_en: I know a singer, and her name is Cocona...

t_ch: 柯柯娜？没听说过，应该不是什么大明星吧……
t_en: Cocona? Never heard of her, so she's probably not some megastar...

t_ch: 不过既然是老板你推荐的，那必定有什么过人之处。快快快，赶紧去把人请过来吧！
t_en: But since you recommend her, there must be something amazing about her. Let's hurry up and invite her!

t_ch: 我…即便您这么说，我还是有点紧张……
t_en: I... Even with your reassurance, I'm still a little bit worried...

t_ch: 别怕，我是你最忠诚的听众。
t_en: Don't be afraid. I'm your most avid fan.

t_ch: 大家一定会爱上你的歌声！
t_en: Everyone will love your singing!

t_ch: 我会一刻不停地为你鼓掌~
t_en: I will be applauding non-stop for you!

t_ch: 哈…这情景倒是似曾相识。既然…您如此信任我，我也得努力不辜负您的期望才行……
t_en: Ha... This sight is familiar. Since you trust me this much, I must work harder to meet your expectations...

t_ch: 无论如何，非常感谢您能给我这次机会。
t_en: No matter what, I truly appreciate you for giving me this chance.

t_ch: 在你的盛意邀请下，柯柯娜鼓起勇气站在台上。
t_en: Spurred by your sincere invitation, Cocona musters up her courage and stands on the stage.

t_ch: …我…*深呼吸声*…非常荣幸能够登上这个舞台，接下来我将为大家带来一首歌曲，它的名字叫做「想象一朵未来的玫瑰」……
t_en: ... I...*deep breath*... I'm very honored to be able to stand on this stage. Next up, I have a song for everyone, and it's called "Envision a Rose Forthcoming"...

t_ch: 这人谁啊，哪个小明星吗，我怎么没见过……
t_en: Who's that? Is that some small-time singer? I've never seen her before...

t_ch: 我哪知道，看这个架势不像是什么专业歌手，也不知道哪请来的……
t_en: How would I know? She doesn't look like a professional singer from that pose of hers. Who knows where she got invited from...

t_ch: 「请闭上眼睛，再往前看」
t_en: "Close your eyes and look ahead."

t_ch: 咦，她的声音，好棒啊……
t_en: Ohh, her voice, it's beautiful...

t_ch: 「那些未完成的画，仍在流淌的歌」
t_en: "Those unfinished paintings and the songs that are still playing."

t_ch: 非常独特的音色……
t_en: It sounds so unique...

t_ch: 闭嘴，专心听。
t_en: Shhh, pay attention.

t_ch: 「那缤纷的世界会是什么模样？」
t_en: "What would that vibrant world look like?"

t_ch: 「那里也会有梦吗？」
t_en: "Will there be a dream there too?"

t_ch: 「有深不见底的海吗？」
t_en: "Will there be a fathomless sea?"

t_ch: 「鸟儿会在落日时分歌唱吗？」
t_en: "Will birds sing at sunset?"

t_ch: 「时间的芬芳啊，你闻到了吗？」
t_en: "Can you smell the fragrance of time?"

t_ch: 「迷路的人，向前看，那里并非空无一物」
t_en: "Lost ones, look forth. There lies not void."

t_ch: 「只是那来自未来的玫瑰，还未盛放……」
t_en: "But that rose from future has yet to bloom..."

t_ch: 一曲终了，全场阒然。人们似乎还沉浸在这动人余韵之中，无法回神。
t_en: The song ends, and the audience goes absolutely still. It's as if they are basking in the afterglow of the cadence, reluctant to return to their senses.

t_ch: 良久，单薄的击掌声打破寂静。继而这音响愈发喧嚣浩大，渐渐汇作洪流。
t_en: A long time after, a single person's clapping shatters the deafening silence. And then it gradually grows louder into a rapturous, sonorous tide of applause.

t_ch: 满堂喝彩声中，却见柯柯娜望向你。灯光在她清澈的眼中洒满了星星。
t_en: Amid the hall of raucous cheers and applause, you see Cocona staring at you. The limelight shining on her highlights the myriad stars in her limpid eyes.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我在流梦礁见过一位女士…
t_en: I met a lady at the Dreamflux Reef...

t_ch: 流梦礁？那是什么地方…听这不像是匹诺康尼的地名，难不成——哇老板你难道还特意为了我们去别的梦境找歌手了吗我真是爱死你了……
t_en: The Dreamflux Reef? What sorta place is that... It doesn't sound like it's on Penacony. Could it be... Woah! Boss, don't tell me you specially made a trip to the other Dreamscapes to look for a singer for us? Gosh, I love you to death...

t_ch: 我不是我没有…
t_en: I'm not. I didn't...

t_ch: 你先冷静一点…
t_en: All right. Calm down first...

t_ch: 好好好我可冷静了冷静的就像是百层圣代…咳、老板你先把人叫来吧！
t_en: Okay okay, I'm super calm now, as calm as a thousand-layered sundae... Ahem. Boss! You should totally get her here first!

t_ch: 可以的可以的，老板你的品味真不错啊。
t_en: Of course, of course, you've definitely got taste, Boss.

t_ch: 邀请流梦礁那位女士。
t_en: Invite that lady from the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 你想起在流梦礁遇见的一位女士，思忖片刻后，你笃定这正是她大显身手的好机会。
t_en: You remember meeting a lady at the Dreamflux Reef. After a moment's contemplation, you decide that this is the perfect opportunity for her to flaunt her talents.

t_ch: 在你的主动邀请下，她答应前来表演。
t_en: On your invitation, she agrees to perform.

t_ch: 她开始轻声哼唱……
t_en: She starts humming a song without music...

t_ch: 她的歌声仿佛带有某种吸引力，观众席上一片寂静，所有人的目光都集中在她身上。
t_en: It is as if her voice has some certain allure — the audience is a sea of silence. All eyes are focused on her.

t_ch: 一曲结束，观众们如梦初醒，忙不迭地连声喝彩。
t_en: The song ends, and the audience rouses from a seeming dream, scrambling to pay tribute to her with applause.

t_ch: 台上人定定地望着你，你感觉到某种真挚的谢意。
t_en: The person on the stage soulfully stares at you. You feel a certain appreciative sincerity from those eyes.

t_ch: 看看有没有你要的谱子…
t_en: Let's see if there're any scores you want...

t_ch: 哇老板就是老板！你真是我见过最好的老板！来来来你想听哪首随便选——
t_en: Woah! That's why you're the boss! You're the best boss I've ever worked for! Come come, what'd you wanna listen to? Pick anyone!

t_ch: 《午夜特快》
t_en: "The Midnight Special"

t_ch: 《嗨，多莉》
t_en: "Hi, Dolly!"

t_ch: 《乐园陌影》
t_en: "Stranger Than Paradise"

t_ch: 你能表演下你们的拿手曲目吗？
t_en: Can you perform the song you're the most famous for?

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 没问题，那老板你有空多回来看看啊！
t_en: No problem. Come back more often when you have time, Boss!

t_ch: 稍等…容我们排演一下……
t_en: Just one moment... Let us rehearse a little first...

t_ch: 当然，老板请稍等，我们去准备一下……
t_en: Sure thing. Please hang on for a moment while we make some preparations...

t_ch: 好的好的，谢谢老板！
t_en: Of course! Thanks, boss!

t_ch: 她的歌声清澈又温柔，观众们听得如痴如醉，连大气都不敢喘，生怕干扰到她的演出。
t_en: Enthralled by her limpid and mellow singing, the audience find themselves holding their breaths, for fear of spoiling her performance.

t_ch: 随着最后一个尾音的落下，观众们纷纷要求下再来一首，歌者有点腼腆地表示「下次一定」，观众们这才罢休。
t_en: The audience erupts into an encore chant right after she sings her final note, quieting down only after the singer shyly promises to do so next time.

t_ch: 啊！老板，您来了！今天的表演，您心里可有合适的人选？需要帮您安排起来吗？
t_en: Ah! Boss, you're here. Do you have anyone in mind for today's performance? I can help you make the arrangements.

t_ch: 就这些了…
t_en: That's it for now.

t_ch: 啊，船长！揽收处那边收到一个您的包裹，我这就给您送来了。
t_en: Ah, Captain! A package arrived for you at the collection point. I've brought it over.

t_ch: 我看您不在里面，我还在想直接放里面是否有点不合适…正好您来了，哈哈。
t_en: I didn't see you, so I was wondering if it'd be okay to leave it inside... Haha, but you came at the right time.

t_ch: 啊，船长！揽收处那边收到一个您的包裹，我这就给您送来了。
t_en: Ah, Captain! A package arrived for you at the collection point. I've brought it over.

t_ch: 我看您不在里面，我还在想直接放里面是否有点不合适…正好您来了，哈哈。
t_en: I didn't see you, so I was wondering if it'd be okay to leave it inside... Haha, but you came at the right time.

t_ch: 啊，船长！揽收处那边收到一个您的包裹，我这就给您送来了。
t_en: Ah, Captain! A package arrived for you at the collection point. I've brought it over.

t_ch: 我看您不在里面，我还在想直接放里面是否有点不合适…正好您来了，哈哈。
t_en: I didn't see you, so I was wondering if it'd be okay to leave it inside... Haha, but you came at the right time.

t_ch: 收下包裹。
t_en: Accept the package.

t_ch: 我就先不打扰您了，有什么问题请随时联系我。
t_en: I'll leave you to it now. Call for me if you need anything!

t_ch: 我就先不打扰您了，有什么问题请随时联系我。
t_en: I'll leave you to it now. Call for me if you need anything!

t_ch: 我就先不打扰您了，有什么问题请随时联系我。
t_en: I'll leave you to it now. Call for me if you need anything!

t_ch: 包裹封口完好，包裹表面并无破损或污渍，寄送信息上清楚地写了你的名字，寄件人则是「砂金」，并细心地附带了星际和平公司的防伪标识。
t_en: The package is neither damaged nor dirty, and has its seal intact. Your name is printed clearly in the recipient field on the waybill, with Aventurine listed as the sender. The Interastral Peace Corporation's mark of authenticity has also been carefully made visible on the package.

t_ch: 你下意识地松了口气，确信这并不是哪位假面愚者的恶作剧——虽然你在拆开包裹前还是无法断言……
t_en: You subconsciously let out a sigh of relief, now knowing that this is not a prank by one of those Masked Fools... Though you cannot be absolutely certain either, at least not until the contents of the package are revealed.

t_ch: …罢了，事已至此，先开箱吧。
t_en: ...Oh, whatever. It is what it is. Just open the package.

t_ch: 打开包裹。
t_en: Open the package.

t_ch: 包裹里塞满了匹诺康尼特产。你再三回忆，确信某些星球会将这种无故投递礼物的行为称为「投喂」，当地传统将之视为友谊的象征。
t_en: The package is stuffed full of Penacony specialties. After thinking about it for a while, you are certain that the natives of some planets call the unprompted act of delivering gifts "feeding." According to local customs, such behavior is viewed as a symbol of friendship.

t_ch: 翻找片刻后，你又在重重精美小礼品之下发现了一张纸条，上面贴着一枚…硬币？
t_en: After a brief search, you discover a note sitting beneath the layers of exquisite gifts. It seems that a... coin has been taped onto it?

t_ch: 原来是艾迪恩代币，而不是其他什么值钱的贵金属——真可惜，但至少这下你的牙不会遭殃了。
t_en: Turns out the coin is merely an Aideen Token and not some other valuable metal trinket. A pity, but at least there's no need for you to bite into the coin now.

t_ch: 你拿起纸条，发觉上面写着一行字：「我用这枚硬币在扭蛋机里连中七次头等奖，希望它能把好运带给你。」
t_en: You pick up the note, upon which the following has been written: "Using this coin, I won the capsule machine's grand prize seven times in a row. Hopefully, you'll be blessed with its good luck as well."

t_ch: 毫无疑问，这是公司使节「砂金」的亲笔签名，千真万确。
t_en: This note was clearly signed by the IPC representative Aventurine himself, without a shadow of a doubt.

t_ch: 把玩硬币，尝试提升幸运值。
t_en: Play with the coin and try to increase your luck value.

t_ch: 很可惜，在你如今所在的世界里没有那种奇妙的量化标准。但你握住了这枚硬币，好似握住了整个银河的气运，心中平白多了不少底气。
t_en: Unfortunately, miraculous quantitative attributes such as a "luck value" do not actually exist in the world that you're currently in. Nevertheless, you grip the coin tightly, almost as though you are holding the entire cosmos's fate in the palm of your hand. Doing so fills you with confidence.

t_ch: 你明白自己是多么武艺高强、风华绝代——硬要说缺点什么，那恐怕就只是那么一点*运气*了。你开始畅想，未来的某一天，你是如何靠这枚硬币赢下整个匹诺康尼……
t_en: You are innately aware of just how powerful and talented you are. However, if you really have to name a weakness of yours, it would be that you are just a little bit unlucky. You start to imagine a day in the future when you'll use this single coin to win the whole of Penacony for yourself...

t_ch: 你很想对砂金致以某种谢意，但基于你一以贯之的高贵教养——用普通话说叫「社恐」——你最终决定还是不去打扰他了。
t_en: You wish to thank Aventurine in some way, but due to your consistent noble upbringing — otherwise known as social anxiety — you ultimately decide not to bother him at all.

t_ch: 正所谓「做兄弟，在心中」…如此为他着想的好意，你相信砂金一定会心领的。
t_en: Given that people say that there is an unspoken bond between true pals... you believe that Aventurine will understand your thoughtfulness without you expressing it.

t_ch: 看看还能找到什么好东西。
t_en: Search the package for more goodies.

t_ch: 真不错！还有什么好东西？
t_en: Perfect! What other goodies will you be able to find?

t_ch: 你继续翻找包裹，寻找是否有遗漏的地方。果然，在包裹的最底部，你发现了另一个包装精美的礼物盒。好一个「套娃」！就是不知道里面包了些什么。
t_en: You continue to rummage through the package, checking to see if there's anything you missed. Sure enough, at the bottom of the package, you find another elegantly wrapped gift box. Ooh, like nesting dolls! All that's left to do is to find out what's inside.

t_ch: 会是金条吗？如果是砂金，倒也正常。直觉告诉你，他和你在空间站认识的某位富裕人士一样，能带动身边的朋友一起富裕……
t_en: Gold bars, perhaps? That sounds like something Aventurine would give others. Your intuition tells you that he's like another rich person that you met aboard the space station, since both of them share their wealth with their friends...

t_ch: 金条！我来了，金条！
t_en: Gold bars, here I come!

t_ch: 看看他到底在卖什么关子。
t_en: Find out exactly what his surprise is.

t_ch: （这是…星穹列车？星穹列车的微缩模型？！）
t_en: (This is... the Astral Express? No, it's actually a miniature model of the Astral Express!?)

t_ch: 和那枚硬币一样，盒子又附带了一张手写的卡片：「以此小礼，恭贺星穹列车喜提『{TEXTJOIN#87}』。」
t_en: As with the coin, a handwritten card has been placed in this box: "I'd like to congratulate the Astral Express on acquiring {TEXTJOIN#87} with this little gift."

t_ch: （既然是恭贺「星穹列车」的，还是跟大家说一声吧……）
t_en: (Given that this gift was meant to congratulate the Astral Express, you should let the others know about this as well...)

t_ch: （不知道包裹里是什么呢…还是过去签收一下吧，万一放过期了就尴尬了……）
t_en: (I wonder what's in the package... I'd better go sign for it. It'll be so awkward if I only pick it up after the hold period.)

t_ch: （大副帮我领了包裹还是回船长室一趟，找大副问问吧。）
t_en: (The first mate collected the package for me. I'll ask for it in the Captain's Quarters.)

t_ch: …船长的快递到了。
t_en: The captain's delivery is here.

t_ch: …船长的快递到了。
t_en: The captain's delivery is here.

t_ch: …船长的快递到了。
t_en: The captain's delivery is here.

t_ch: 看上去像片场的远光灯，又有点像某种古老的投影设备，你不确定它究竟是什么。
t_en: It looks like high-beam lights used on set, but it could also be an old-fashioned projecting device. You're not sure what it is.

t_ch: 你注意到它的机身上有一个显著的按钮，你决定——
t_en: You notice a conspicuous button on its body. You decide to...

t_ch: 狠狠地按下去。
t_en: Press it with conviction.

t_ch: 你试着按下按钮，但那个镜头一样的玩意并没有如你所期望的那样亮起。
t_en: You press the button, but the lens-like component doesn't light up like you expected it to.

t_ch: 或许这台机器只是个摆设——你开始思考在你脑海中自然形成的这个推论是否准确。
t_en: Maybe the machine is just for show. You begin to wonder whether this conclusion your natural powers of deduction brought you to is correct.

t_ch: 就在大脑飞速运转时，你听见一阵急促的脚步声，夹杂着某种气喘吁吁的声音在向你靠近。
t_en: As your mind works overtime, you hear rapid footsteps and the sound of ragged breathing approaching.

t_ch: 一位侍从急匆匆地朝你跑了过来，将某个东西放在你身旁。
t_en: A frantic attendant comes running toward you and places something down next to you.

t_ch: 算了，还是别乱碰了。
t_en: Never mind. I'll stop fiddling with it.

t_ch: 您想启动放映机吗？
t_en: Would you like to use the projector?

t_ch: 您、您好…呼…呼呼…请问您是想启动放映机吗？
t_en: H—Hello! *huff* *huff* Are you here to use the projector?

t_ch: 你还是先缓缓吧。
t_en: Slow down and catch your breath first.

t_ch: 呼呼——
t_en: Whew...

t_ch: 现在已经好多啦，抱歉，最近都没怎么锻炼，没想到跑两步就累，让您见笑了。
t_en: Thanks, I feel much better now. Sorry about that, I haven't been getting much exercise lately. Didn't expect to wear myself out from such a short run. How embarrassing.

t_ch: 对，有什么电影看吗？
t_en: Uh-huh. What movies do you have?

t_ch: 电影？抱歉，这台放映机是放不了电影的……
t_en: Movies? I'm sorry, but this projector doesn't play movies.

t_ch: 请容我为您介绍一下——这台放映机与一般的放映机不同。它不是用来放电影的，而是用来播放使用者的「回忆片段」的。
t_en: Allow me to explain: This device here isn't your average projector. It doesn't play movies. Instead, it displays a segment of the user's memories.

t_ch: 您瞧这里，这里是用来囤放压缩过的空白梦泡。只要把手搭在那儿，就能记录下使用者过往的精彩回忆。
t_en: If you'll look over here, this is where blank compressed dream bubbles are loaded. Just place your hand here and wonderful moments from your past will be saved into the dream bubbles.

t_ch: …不愧是匹诺康尼。
t_en: Penacony never fails to impress.

t_ch: 是的，这台放映机在匹诺康尼也算是抢手货。
t_en: Indeed. This projector is a hot commodity even in Penacony.

t_ch: 这么神奇？试试我的回忆吧！
t_en: That sounds fascinating. Let me try!

t_ch: 好的，压缩梦泡已经装填完毕，您可以——
t_en: Certainly. I've loaded a compressed dream bubble. You can—

t_ch: 等等，忘记跟您说了…目前可供放映的剩余次数仅有一次。因为压缩梦泡的制作不易，成品是限量供应的。目前我们这儿只剩下一盘，其他的还在等待发货。
t_en: Ah, I almost forgot to mention this, but we can only do one projection currently. Manufacturing compressed dream bubbles is a highly demanding process, so there's only a limited supply available. We only have one bubble left now. The rest will take time to ship.

t_ch: 您确定要独自使用吗？一般来说，这个房间是「家庭放映厅」，沙发也是为五人以上的团体使用所准备的。
t_en: Are you sure you want to use it alone? This is normally the family screening room. Even the sofa is made to fit more than five people.

t_ch: 机会难得，您是否要邀请其他贵客前来，与您共同观赏回忆放映？
t_en: This opportunity doesn't come by often. Would you like to invite any guests to watch the memories with you?

t_ch: 邀请，狠狠地邀请！
t_en: Yes! Send the invites to 'em all!

t_ch: 让我再想想吧。
t_en: I need some time to process all that information.

t_ch: 好的，请稍等。
t_en: Understood. One moment, please.

t_ch: 请问您想邀请哪几位住客呢？我这就帮您去邀请他们过来。
t_en: Which guests on board would you like to invite? I'll send for them right away.

t_ch: 就只能是住客吗？
t_en: Does it have to be a guest on board?

t_ch: …啊，抱歉，我以为您想邀请的亲朋也在空艇上，是我的理解出现了偏差。
t_en: Oh, my apologies. I'd assumed the friends you'd like to invite were also on board. I'm sorry for the misunderstanding.

t_ch: …你确定你能联系到他们吗？
t_en: You're able to get into contact with them?

t_ch: …呃，在空艇上的话就没问题。如果是在其他地方，恐怕…不行。
t_en: Uh... Only if they're also on board the airship. If not... I'm afraid my abilities are limited.

t_ch: 非常抱歉，我先前有些考虑不周了。如果您想邀请的宾客尚处于匹诺康尼境内，请您先行联络他们。在确认邀请的宾客后，我会把您的宾客名录上报至空艇招待部门处。
t_en: My apologies, I should've taken that into account. If the guests you wish to invite are still in Penacony, please get in touch with them first. Once you're sure of whom to invite, I'll submit the guest list to the airship's front desk.

t_ch: 届时将有专人带他们登陆空艇。期间若是有什么服务问题的话，请向空艇上任一工作人员反馈。
t_en: Someone will then bring them aboard the airship. If you encounter any problems in the course of our service, you may approach any of our staff on board for help.

t_ch: 自行联络宾客也不是件坏事，起码你不会担心被侍从听到冰冷的忙音。
t_en: Contacting your guests yourself isn't so bad. At least you don't have to worry about the attendants having to deal with busy signals.

t_ch: 在思忖片刻后，你想起现在正身处匹诺康尼的朋友们…你决定——
t_en: After giving it some thought, you think of your friends in Penacony. You decide to...

t_ch: 邀请星穹列车全员！
t_en: Invite the entire Astral Express crew!

t_ch: 你往「星穹列车一家人」群组中发送了活动邀请。
t_en: You send an invitation to "The Astral Express Family" group chat.

t_ch: 等待片刻后……
t_en: A few moments later...

t_ch: 瓦尔特正在一处无人的角落观看手机里的故乡动画，他第一时间响应了你并表示随后就到。
t_en: Welt is on his phone in a secluded corner, watching some animation videos from his homeworld. He replies to you immediately and promises to be there soon.

t_ch: 姬子与三月七正在「黄金的时刻」商店街购物，欣然接受了邀请，并表示逛完就来，可能会晚点到。
t_en: Himeko and March 7th, who are shopping at the Golden Hour, happily accept the invite and promise to be there once they're done. They'll probably be a little late.

t_ch: 帕姆一口气发了六个带有问号的表情包来答复你，丹恒以留下来看守列车为由拒绝了你，并解释说帕姆因为情绪太过激动才会手抖。
t_en: Pom-Pom replies with a flurry of six stickers with question marks. Dan Heng declines, citing that someone needs to stay behind and watch over the Express. He also explains that Pom-Pom's sticker spam was just a result of being overexcited.

t_ch: 邀请流萤！
t_en: Invite Firefly!

t_ch: 你拨打了流萤的号码，但冰冷的电子音回答你：「对方不在服务区」 。
t_en: You dial Firefly's number, only to hear a cold robotic voice: This person is currently outside of the service area.

t_ch: 邀请公司的朋友！
t_en: Invite your friends from the IPC!

t_ch: 你想起在匹诺康尼认识的砂金，你邀请砂金通话，但对方的状态为「正在开会」，勿扰模式自动切断了你的来电。
t_en: You think of Aventurine whom you met in Penacony and decide to call him. His status, however, indicates that he's "in a meeting," and the "Do Not Disturb" feature automatically ends the call.

t_ch: 你注意到托帕也是同样的状态，抱着不死心的想法你再次向托帕发起邀请，但致电却被勿扰模式给拦截了。
t_en: You notice that Topaz's status is the same. You stubbornly send the invite to Topaz too, but your call is once again disconnected by the "Do Not Disturb" feature.

t_ch: 邀请花火！
t_en: Invite Sparkle!

t_ch: 你想起一位假面愚者，与她的相识大大提升了你在匹诺康尼的开拓「风险」。
t_en: You think of a certain Masked Fool who made your Trailblazing expedition in Penacony a great deal more dangerous after you two met.

t_ch: 可你认为相识一场既是有缘，你决定将活动邀请发送给她。
t_en: Still, you believe there must be a reason for every encounter. You decide to send her an invitation.

t_ch: 花火立即回复了你，她「贴心」地建议送几个「有趣」的娃娃至空艇上，以便代替自己陪伴你，你联想到一些不妙的事，闻言赶紧拒绝。
t_en: Sparkle replies instantly, very kindly offering to send some "interesting" dolls to the airship to accompany you in her place. The prospect naturally doesn't bode well in your eyes, and you quickly decline her offer.

t_ch: 邀请匹诺康尼本地朋友！
t_en: Invite your Penaconian friends!

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼你认识了不少本地朋友，带他们来见识一下这艘空艇的奢华生活似乎也是不错的选择。
t_en: You have befriended several Penaconians since your arrival. Inviting them for a luxurious experience on board the airship seems like a pretty nice idea.

t_ch: 在你向舒翁等人发送邀请后，他们陆续以「还要继续工作」为由拒绝了你，你有些无奈，却又不好意思影响人家的生计，只得无奈应下。
t_en: You send invites to Siobhan and the others, but all of them decline with the same reason: "I have to work." Though crestfallen, you know you shouldn't get in their way of making a living, so you accept the rejections graciously.

t_ch: 你向侍从确认了你的邀请名单，在耐心的等待后，瓦尔特、姬子与三月七赶到了你身边。
t_en: You pass the attendant the guest list and wait patiently. A short while later, Welt, Himeko, and March 7th show up.

t_ch: 在哪儿在哪儿？就是用这个看电影吗？
t_en: Where is it? Where is it? Do we use this thing here to watch the movie?

t_ch: 请容我为大家介绍一下——
t_en: Allow me to explain...

t_ch: 侍从为无名客们介绍起放映机的使用方法和注意事项。
t_en: The attendant explains to the Nameless how the projector works and the relevant things to take note of.

t_ch: …放映机的原理…看上去和流光忆庭的技术有一定关联。
t_en: ...The mechanics of this projector seem rather similar to the technology used by the Garden of Recollection.

t_ch: 要放映的话，只需把掌心轻抵在已装填好的压缩梦泡上即可。
t_en: To start the projection, just press your palm gently against the compressed dream bubble loaded within.

t_ch: 接下来，我就不打扰诸位了，请好好享受吧。如有需要，随时喊我，我就在门外恭候。
t_en: If there's nothing else, I'll take my leave now. Please enjoy yourselves. Feel free to call for me anytime if you need me. I'll be right outside.

t_ch: 还剩最后一盘，{NICKNAME}，你想好要放什么了吗？
t_en: It's their last one, {NICKNAME}. Have you thought about what you're gonna put inside?

t_ch: 可别放太多噩梦进去，咱受不了。
t_en: Don't put too much nightmarish stuff, though! I doubt I can handle that.

t_ch: 压缩梦泡？我想，放映的内容不见得是{NICKNAME}能自由控制的。
t_en: Hmm, a compressed dream bubble. I don't think the choice of projected content is necessarily within {NICKNAME}'s control.

t_ch: 那要是放到某些{NICKNAME}不想公开的场景，岂不是…岂不是尴尬了！
t_en: But what if it plays something {NICKNAME} doesn't want anyone to see? Th—That would be so embarrassing!

t_ch: 放映机上有几个播放相关的设置，我想应该是通过这个来控制梦泡的播放。
t_en: The projector has a few settings for the projection. You can likely control what the dream bubble shows with them.

t_ch: 好吧，那{NICKNAME}你到时候跟我说一声哦，要是有什么不该看的画面，咱马上就把眼睛闭起来！
t_en: Alright, then. Well, just give me a holler if there's something I shouldn't see, {NICKNAME}. I'll shut my eyes immediately!

t_ch: 嗯，还是交由{NICKNAME}自己来操作吧，该看什么{F#她}{M#他}心里清楚。
t_en: Hmm, we should leave the controls to {NICKNAME}. {F#She}{M#He} would know best what to show us.

t_ch: 三月七、姬子与瓦尔特朝你笑了笑，姬子示意你开始放映。
t_en: March 7th, Himeko, and Welt smile at you, and Himeko gestures for you to begin.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 大大方方地播就是了！
t_en: Just do it!

t_ch: 谨慎地接触。
t_en: Touch it cautiously.

t_ch: 你将手掌抵在放映机的尾部，与压缩梦泡亲密接触，纷乱的记忆涌上心头……
t_en: You place your palm against the back of the projector, in close contact with the compressed dream bubble. Chaotic memories flood your mind...

t_ch: 过往的回忆清晰地投映了出来……
t_en: A memory from the past is projected vividly...

t_ch: 随着画面的展开，你看到了自己，握着你在贝洛伯格所获得的那柄骑枪，站在同伴们的最前面，眼神坚定无比。
t_en: As the scene unfolds, you see yourself standing in front of your companions, holding the lance you acquired in Belobog. Your eyes are filled with resolve.

t_ch: 你愕然，究竟是骑枪带给你真实的温暖，抑或是星穹列车的同伴们带给你的暖意，抑或是身旁那位一刀足以斩碎夜空的黄泉…你无从得知，你只记得在心跳加速时那份暖意不曾散去。
t_en: You're stunned. Did that sensation of warmth come from your lance, your companions from the Astral Express, or from the woman whose blade could slice through the night sky — Acheron? You don't know. You only know that the warmth was there while your heart was racing.

t_ch: …我还是很在意，黄泉小姐的实力。
t_en: ...I am still very curious about Miss Acheron's power.

t_ch: 难得看到小三月露出这副表情，挺凝重的。
t_en: It's rare to see March with such a serious expression.

t_ch: 这种危急时刻，虽说有姬子和杨叔在，但也不敢掉以轻心！该靠谱的时候咱还是很靠谱的！
t_en: It was a really dangerous moment. Even though Himeko and Mr. Yang were there, I didn't dare let my guard down. I can be reliable when I need to be!

t_ch: 不过…现在回看…有点奇怪，为什么{NICKNAME}的记忆会是这种旁观的角度？
t_en: Although looking back now... That's weird. How come we're looking at {NICKNAME}'s memory in the third person's perspective?

t_ch: 是手机，我开了录像模式！
t_en: I enabled video recording from my phone!

t_ch: 别开玩笑啦{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Don't be silly, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: …我也不知道。
t_en: ...I don't know either.

t_ch: 确实奇怪。
t_en: How strange.

t_ch: 不过，这里可是匹诺康尼…或许是家族那边的某种独门技艺吧。
t_en: But this is Penacony. It could be a technique unique to The Family.

t_ch: 也是，毕竟是在梦里，什么奇景都有可能发生。
t_en: That's true. We're in a dream, after all. Anything could happen.

t_ch: 还有别的吗，{NICKNAME}，我感觉还没看够呢！
t_en: Do you have anything else, {NICKNAME}? I feel like I haven't seen enough yet!

t_ch: 在同伴们闲谈时，你放空大脑，这段时间经历了太多事，好几段回忆涌上心头。
t_en: While your companions chat, you empty your mind. So much has happened recently, and many memories flood your mind.

t_ch: 该放映哪一段好呢？
t_en: Which memory to project?

t_ch: 记忆深处的恐惧。
t_en: A deeply terrifying memory.

t_ch: 一种无可抵挡的恐惧感涌现，就像不带任何防寒设施在贝洛伯格的风雪里罚站，寒意自脚底上升至眉心。
t_en: An indescribable terror surges forth with a chill that spreads from the soles of your feet to the top of your head. It's as if you are standing in the middle of a Belobogian blizzard without any equipment.

t_ch: 你阖上眼，流萤坠落至你怀中却清晰地放映了出来——就连坠落时她飞舞的发丝，与微风拂过的衣角，一切的一切都如此清晰。
t_en: You close your eyes and the scene of Firefly falling into your arms is projected — the image so vivid you can see the breeze disturbing the edges of her dress and each strand of her hair dancing in the air as she falls.

t_ch: 无从预知，无处抵挡…内心深处好像有某种声音，盖过了一切理智。从什么时候起，向来不知天高地厚的开拓者也会害怕了？你沉默着不愿再想下去。
t_en: Unpredictable, horrifying... A noise from deep in your heart obscures all rational thought. When did the fearless Trailblazer discover what it means to be afraid? You fall silent and refuse to think anymore.

t_ch: 三月七迅速捂住了嘴，盖过了自己的惊呼，然后又捂住了眼睛。
t_en: March 7th covers her mouth as she cries out in surprise, then quickly covers her eyes.

t_ch: …这是…流萤小姐…那次意外？
t_en: ...Is that... Miss Firefly? When she...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你还好吗？抱歉，那时没能陪在你身旁，还好…现在也不迟。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you all right? I'm sorry that I couldn't be with you then, but at least I'm here now.

t_ch: 姬子握住了你的手，体温传来的温暖令你回过神来。
t_en: Himeko holds your hand, the warmth from her own restoring you to your senses.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…你要是不舒服的话，还是先按暂停吧？这玩意能切掉吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you're not feeling well, maybe we should take a break. Is there a stop button on this thing?

t_ch: 你还记得吗？{NICKNAME}，后来发生的事——流萤小姐并没有「死亡」，别怕，过去的事就让它过去吧。
t_en: Remember what happened afterward, {NICKNAME}. Miss Firefly didn't "die." Don't be afraid. Let the past stay in the past.

t_ch: 灵魂根源的渴望。
t_en: A yearning of the soul.

t_ch: 长久以来，你都无法克制住自己那股难以名状的渴望——对于为世人所不容的垃圾桶，你竟生出了无法解释的喜爱与好奇心。
t_en: For as long as you can remember, you've never been able to hold back this yearning. You've developed an indescribable love and curiosity for the object of mankind's repulsion — the trashcan.

t_ch: 这份感情既模糊又强烈，在你来到匹诺康尼之后，它更是超越了理智的边界，令你不能自已。
t_en: This vague yet intense feeling has transcended the boundaries of rationality after you arrived in Penacony. You just can't help yourself.

t_ch: 博识学会的学者认为，梦境是人内心的反馈。在匹诺康尼这片梦中世界里，你常常会见到一个发生垃圾桶王国的故事：关于「垃圾之王」塔塔洛夫，关于桑博，以及一场拳拳到肉的精彩战斗。
t_en: The scholars of the Intelligentsia Guild believe the Dreamscape is a reflection of mankind's heart. In Penacony's dream world, you often witness this story from the Trashcan Kingdom: It's a tale about the Garbage King Tatalov, of Sampo, and of an awesome hard-hitting battle.

t_ch: 你从未想到会有此情此景，原来那些梦中发生的故事竟可以投映出来……
t_en: You didn't expect to see this scene projected here. It appears the projector can show things that happened in a dream, too.

t_ch: 唔…你还真是喜欢垃圾桶啊，满脑子都是垃圾桶……
t_en: Mm... You really like trashcans, huh? Your mind is just full of trashcans....

t_ch: 战斗画面挺热血的，年轻人的想象力果然丰富，匹诺康尼这个地方还真适合你。
t_en: What an intense combat scenario. Penacony truly is the perfect place for youngsters with a rich imagination like yours.

t_ch: 有趣的爱好…{NICKNAME}，需要帮你按下跳过键吗？
t_en: Interesting hobby, {NICKNAME}... Shall I press the skip button for you?

t_ch: 同伴给予的安全感。
t_en: A sense of security from your comrades.

t_ch: 你想起了在剧院直面星期日的事…在诗班奏响未来的序曲前，空气凝固，时间似乎都静止了下来。
t_en: You remember when you faced Sunday at the theater. The choir was about to sing the prelude to the future, the air froze, and time seemed to stop.

t_ch: 然而，就在这千钧一发之际，仙舟联盟的突然出现，以雷霆万钧之势出现，清除了一切危机。
t_en: Then, just in the nick of time, the Xianzhou Alliance appeared, ending the crisis with overwhelming force.

t_ch: 这是…梦吗？你看到了那个瑰丽的场景，丹恒与景元联手，令恐惧和不安在这一刻消散，某种难以言喻的安全感笼罩了你。
t_en: Is this... a dream? You look at the magnificent scene of Dan Heng and Jing Yuan working in tandem. Their presence dissipating your fear and unease in the moment, enveloping you with a sense of safety and security.

t_ch: 尽管时至今日，你都很难把丹恒与他的秘密身份联系到一起……
t_en: Even to this day, you still have a hard time thinking of Dan Heng and his secret identity as the same person...

t_ch: 快看，是丹恒耶！还有景元将军！好帅气的亮相！{NICKNAME}，咱在你脑海里怎么就没这种待遇啊！
t_en: Look, it's Dan Heng! And General Jing Yuan! They're so dashing! {NICKNAME}, why don't I look like that in your memories?

t_ch: 当时…星期日先生并没有对我下狠手，只是把我囚禁在意识中，多亏了景元将军赶来，我才能逃出生天。
t_en: Mr. Sunday did not deal with me too harshly at the time. He only imprisoned me in the consciousness. It's thanks to General Jing Yuan that I managed to escape.

t_ch: 没想到景元将军出手的场景如此震撼，没能亲眼见证实在可惜。
t_en: I never imagined General Jing Yuan's appearance would be so astounding. It's a shame I couldn't see it for myself.

t_ch: 这段时间发生了这么多事，{NICKNAME}，你还是找个机会好好休养一下吧。虽说现在平安无事，但要是落下什么心理阴影的，以后可就遭罪了。
t_en: So much has happened in such a short time, {NICKNAME}. You should take some time for yourself. We might be safe now, but we should make sure there isn't any lasting psychological trauma.

t_ch: 给自己留点隐私，就不放映了。
t_en: I'd like to keep something for myself, I'll pass.

t_ch: 你再三思索，基于保护隐私的理念，你不想在同伴面前暴露你的个人生活，你决定还是为自己留几分体面吧。
t_en: You think it over and decide for the sake of your own privacy not to expose your personal life to your companions. You decide to maintain some sense of dignity for yourself.

t_ch: 在瑰丽的梦境中，你分不清什么是真，什么是假。但无论发生什么变故，你的同伴始终在你身旁陪伴着你，这就够了。
t_en: You can't tell what is real and what is fake in this magnificent Dreamscape, but no matter what happens, your companions will always be by your side. That's enough for you.

t_ch: 于是你决定，不去想这些就好了。
t_en: And so, you decide not to think about it anymore.

t_ch: 不知不觉间，{NICKNAME}也成长起来了，现在都能独当一面了。
t_en: It seems that {NICKNAME} has matured more than we had realized and is much more independent now.

t_ch: 有点可惜，刚刚看得太投入，忘记拍照留念了！这算是{NICKNAME}主演的大电影吧，这种场合不跟主角合照也太可惜了！
t_en: What a pity! I was too engrossed just now and forgot to take a picture to commemorate the moment! This is a big movie with {NICKNAME} in the leading role, right? In that case, it'd be too bad if we don't take a photo with the star of the movie!

t_ch: 要是还有压缩梦泡就好了，这种精彩的画面，重映多少次我都不会腻！
t_en: If only we had a compressed dream bubble. I wouldn't be bored with such an exciting moment, no matter how many times they're replayed!

t_ch: 珍贵的回忆看一次就够了，沉湎其中也不是好事，还是向前看吧，小三月？
t_en: Precious memories should only be viewed once because it's not good to overindulge in such things. March, how about we look toward the future, instead?

t_ch: 还有…{NICKNAME}，你也是。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... This applies to you, as well.

t_ch: 放心，我听劝。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll listen to your advice.

t_ch: 嗯，这就是开拓精神！
t_en: Ahh, such is the spirit of trailblazing!

t_ch: 姬子对你笑了笑，温柔地表示了某种肯定，你确信你感受到了她对你的某种期许。
t_en: Himeko gently smiles at you in affirmation. You feel as though she has high expectations of you.

t_ch: 你与同伴们闲聊了一会儿，在温情时刻结束后，彼此道别，然后短暂分开。
t_en: After chatting with your companions for a while longer, the heartwarming moment comes to an end. You bid them goodbye, and they leave you for the time being.

t_ch: 此处只剩你一人，你留在原地，恍惚之间以为<color=#dbc291ff>方才的一切是场幻梦</color>。
t_en: At long last, only you are left standing in the same spot. In a daze, you seem to feel as though <color=#dbc291ff>everything that had happened just now was just a dream</color>.

t_ch: 一间无主牢房孤零零地恭候着你的莅临，犹如无主的王国等待着它的君主。这里原本应关押着穷凶极恶的囚徒，如今空空如也。
t_en: An empty cell awaits you, much like an unoccupied kingdom awaits its ruler. It was once meant to confine the most notorious prisoner, yet it now stands vacant.

t_ch: 虽然这么比喻对囚徒不太合适——就像是你去了动物园，满心期待热门动物在园区视野最好的地方被你观察，可是它却不知为何躲在某块大石头下面睡觉，什么也看不着。
t_en: This analogy doesn't quite fit for prisoners, but it's like a visit to the zoo — you eagerly anticipate seeing the star attraction in the prime viewing area, only to find it hidden and asleep beneath a boulder, leaving you nothing to see.

t_ch: 要不，再看一会吧？
t_en: How about observing for a little while longer?

t_ch: 事实上，你意识到了，若无其事地继续看下去就会有什么事情发生……
t_en: As a matter of fact, you already realize what would happen if you continue looking at it...

t_ch: 放弃，差不多得了。
t_en: Let's just give up. It's about time to go.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人？
t_en: {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 你转过身去，发现这是一位你从未打过照面的狱卒，但是她轻松地叫出了你的名字，也许就是成为名人的烦恼吧。
t_en: As you turn around, you're met by a prison guard you've never encountered before, but she effortlessly calls you by your name. Perhaps that's the trouble with being a celebrity.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，您怎么会在这里？您不是在勘录舍里指导…工作吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why are you here? Aren't you supposed to be at the Domain of Collations supervising... their work?

t_ch: 勘录舍…是什么地方？
t_en: Domain of Collations... what is that place?

t_ch: 您刚刚不就在那里吗？我才从勘录舍出来……
t_en: Weren't you there just now? I just came from the Domain of Collations...

t_ch: 我指导…十王司工作？
t_en: I supervise... Ten-Lords Commission's work?

t_ch: 您刚刚不就在大谈特谈幽囚狱的防越狱工作不够严谨……
t_en: Weren't you just rattling on about the lack of rigorous control over escape prevention in The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 我们…之前有见过面吗？
t_en: Have we... met before?

t_ch: 您真是贵人多忘事啊，再说，我们也才没分开多久……
t_en: You must be kidding. We just met not long ago...

t_ch: 等等！我已经明白是怎么回事了。刚才我收到监察系统通报，发现「无相者」嘟嘟的牢房出现了安全漏洞警告，现在看来，嘟嘟已经趁乱逃出来了。
t_en: Hold on! Now I get it. I just received a notification from the monitoring system that there was a security breach from the Shapeless Doot-Doot's cell. It seems that Doot-Doot has taken advantage of the chaos to escape.

t_ch: 换句话说，你或者上面那个「你」，其中一个就是「无相者」嘟嘟。
t_en: In other words, either you or the one upstairs is the Shapeless Doot-Doot.

t_ch: 那你们快去抓住它呀！
t_en: Then what are you waiting for? Go and catch it!

t_ch: 你不会…以为我是嘟嘟吧？
t_en: You don't think... I'm Doot-Doot, do you?

t_ch: 狱卒的目光一变，开始对你进行上下打量。
t_en: The prison guard's gaze shifts abruptly, assessing you with keen scrutiny.

t_ch: 想必她也很难把握到底该用什么语气同你说话——要是语气尊敬一些，万一你是嘟嘟怎么办；要是语气严厉一些，万一你是真的{NICKNAME}怎么办？
t_en: She's probably also having a hard time figuring out the appropriate tone with you — what if she's respectful to you, only to find that you're Doot-Doot in disguise? Conversely, what if she adopts a stern demeanor, only to discover you're the genuine {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: …我分辨不出来，你跟我走一趟吧。
t_en: ...I can't tell. You have to come with me.

t_ch: 这是你们自己的事…
t_en: That is your problem...

t_ch: 你继续拒绝的话，我会认为你就是嘟嘟；而你跟我上去，反而会有证明自己的机会——我不是在请求你，我是在要求你。
t_en: If you refuse to come, I'll assume you're Doot-Doot. However, if you choose to join me upstairs, you'll have a chance to prove your identity — consider this not an invitation, but a demand.

t_ch: 走吧，我倒要看看是谁！
t_en: Let's go. I'm curious to find out who's upstairs!

t_ch: 嗯，走吧。
t_en: Yeah, let's go.

t_ch: 你把你们这里的管事的人叫出来，雪衣呢？寒鸦呢？藿藿呢？再不行，就把你们仙舟的神策府将军景元叫过来！
t_en: Get your supervisors here. Xueyi? Hanya? Huohuo? If they are unavailable, request the presence of General Jing Yuan from Xianzhou's Seat of Divine Foresight.

t_ch: …那我去把寒鸦大人叫过来。
t_en: ...Let me get Madam Hanya.

t_ch: 等等！你们这些办事的狱卒，不要拿这样的小事去为难判官，所以说啊，你们的业务水平真的不太行。
t_en: Wait a minute! As prison guards, you shouldn't trouble the judges with such trivial matters. Like I say, your work standard isn't up to par.

t_ch: 我把另一位{NICKNAME}大人带过来了，另外，现在「无相者」嘟嘟已经脱离了囚室，很有可能是这二人其中一人。
t_en: I've escorted the other {NICKNAME} here. Additionally, with the escape of Shapeless Doot-Doot from its cell, there's a chance that either of these two individuals could be Doot-Doot.

t_ch: 没事，这事交给我吧，我有经验。我知道你们都会叫自己{NICKNAME}，这个名称代号已经没有意义了，一直在这里的这个就叫做「嫌疑人甲」。
t_en: No need to worry. Leave this matter to me. I have ample experience. Both of you will just claim to be {NICKNAME}, so asking for your name is meaningless. Let's just call the one who has been here for all this time Suspect A.

t_ch: 而你，刚来的，就叫做「嫌疑人乙」。
t_en: And you who have just arrived, Suspect B.

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}是假的，我是真的。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} is a fake. I'm the real one.

t_ch: …别废话。「你是真的」、「你是假的」…就只是一堆车轱辘话。去，带一只谛听过来。
t_en: ...Save your breath. "You're real," "You're a fake"... These are all pointless. Go and get me a Diting.

t_ch: 是，魂停大人。
t_en: Yes, Judge Hunting.

t_ch: 巡逻的狱卒收到命令后离开，而你明显感觉到那个「嫌疑人甲」有些紧张……
t_en: The patrolling prison guard received the order and leaves. You notice Suspect A is somewhat jittery...

t_ch: 在谛听到来之前，我允许你们向对方发起提问。我对{NICKNAME}也不太熟悉，只会从旁观察，不过判断依据我是不会告诉你们了。
t_en: Before Diting's arrival, you two are free to pose any questions to each other. I'm not acquainted with {NICKNAME}, so I won't intervene. Nevertheless, I won't disclose my ultimate decision.

t_ch: 现在，我给你们一点时间思考，但不能用语言交流。
t_en: Now, I'll give you some time to think, but no verbal communication.

t_ch: 你们不给我好吃好喝的招待着也就算了，怎么我要离开这破地方还这么多事？
t_en: You don't give me a grand welcome, fine, but why do I still have to go through so many hoops just to leave this wretched place?

t_ch: 一样的发色，一样的穿着，一样的瞳色，一样的身高。嫌疑人甲，你最熟悉的陌生人。
t_en: You and the other suspect have the same hair color, outfit, pupil color, and height. Suspect A is your most familiar stranger.

t_ch: 既然他提到了多个不在场判官的名字，想必也是从你那里得到了一些暧昧的记忆，这记忆是否能够以假乱真，就不得而知了。
t_en: Since that individual has listed the names of several absent judges, it must have gotten some vague memories from you, but it's unknown whether these memories are authentic or fabricated.

t_ch: 再好好观察一下「嫌疑人甲」吧，也许对你的提问有利。
t_en: It's best to take some time to observe Suspect A. You may be able to find some clues.

t_ch: 得出什么结论了吗？
t_en: Reached any conclusions?

t_ch: 真是一张伟大的脸。
t_en: What a magnificent face.

t_ch: 正确的，大家都很喜欢你。
t_en: You're right. Many people like you.

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}才是真的{NICKNAME}。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} is the genuine {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 请你对自己有些自信。
t_en: Please be more confident in yourself.

t_ch: 现在可以和自己合影吗？
t_en: Can I take a photo with myself?

t_ch: 当然可以，对话结束之后，请善用相机功能。
t_en: Of course. After the talk, you may make use of the Camera function.

t_ch: 准备好了？
t_en: Are you ready?

t_ch: 快快让我提问。
t_en: Hurry. Let me ask my questions.

t_ch: 我再想想。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 很好，现在我会允许你们互相向对方提出一个问题，对方也必须回答一个问题。鉴于这里已经有一个人正在说谎，我也不指望你们都诚实回答。
t_en: Very well. You're now both granted the opportunity to pose a question to each other, and the respondent must provide an answer. Given that one of you is already lying, I will not expect honest responses to all the questions.

t_ch: 因此，我仅会从你们问了什么问题，如何做出回答来进行判断，当然我不会立刻告诉你们。接下来，就从你开始吧，嫌疑人甲提问。
t_en: Therefore, I'll base my decisions solely on the questions presented and how the answers are given. Naturally, I won't reveal my decisions immediately. Let's begin with you, Suspect A. Pose your question.

t_ch: 我是星穹列车的列车组成员，这问题的答案只有星穹列车的成员才会知道。所以等{F#她}{M#他}回答完之后，十王司可以求证杨叔、姬子姐姐或者帕姆。
t_en: I am a crew member of the Astral Express, and only the Crew holds the answer to this question. The Ten-Lords Commission is welcome to verify the answer with Mr. Yang, Himeko, or Pom-Pom after {F#she}{M#he} has provided an answer.

t_ch: 你所有的记忆都是来自我这里。
t_en: All your memories come from me.

t_ch: 你想说，我知道的都是你知道的？那可不一定哦！
t_en: Are you trying to say you know everything that I know? Don't be too confident!

t_ch: …我们之间也有不知道的秘密。
t_en: ...There are unknown secrets between us too.

t_ch: 冥差大人，{F#她}{M#他}心虚了。
t_en: Your Honor, {F#she}{M#he} is guilty.

t_ch: 提问，星穹列车的观景车厢里有很多的灯，其中落地灯有多少盏呢？
t_en: My question is: You can find many lamps in the Parlor Car of the Astral Express. How many of them are floor lamps?

t_ch: 错了！是4盏！冥差大人，{F#她}{M#他}是假的。
t_en: Incorrect! It's 4! Your Honor, {F#she}{M#he} is a fake.

t_ch: 我说过了，我不会根据正确错误来判断。
t_en: As I've mentioned, I will not judge based on the answers.

t_ch: 你是蒙对的吧？
t_en: That must be a lucky guess, huh?

t_ch: 错了！是4盏！冥差大人，{F#她}{M#他}是假的。
t_en: Incorrect! It's 4! Your Honor, {F#she}{M#he} is a fake.

t_ch: 我说过了，我不会根据正确错误来判断。
t_en: As I've mentioned, I will not judge based on the answers.

t_ch: 错了！是4盏！冥差大人，{F#她}{M#他}是假的。
t_en: Incorrect! It's 4! Your Honor, {F#she}{M#he} is a fake.

t_ch: 我说过了，我不会根据正确错误来判断。
t_en: As I've mentioned, I will not judge based on the answers.

t_ch: 跳过。
t_en: Skip.

t_ch: 没有跳过！你心虚了吧！冥差大人，{F#她}{M#他}是假的。
t_en: You can't skip the question! You're guilty, aren't you!? Your Honor, {F#she}{M#he} is a fake.

t_ch: 我猜正常人应该不会记得这个…
t_en: I don't think any normal being will remember such details...

t_ch: 闭嘴，嫌疑人乙！你说谁不是正常人呢？
t_en: Shut up, Suspect B! Who are you calling abnormal?

t_ch: 我可以高维度作弊，你输定了。
t_en: Any cheat you can do, I can do better. You're going to lose.

t_ch: 闭嘴，嫌疑人乙！只能用作弊的手段，也敢自称星穹列车的无名客吗？
t_en: Shut up, Suspect B! How dare you call yourself the Nameless on Astral Express when all you can do is cheat?

t_ch: 现在该你来提问了，嫌疑人乙。
t_en: Now's your turn to ask a question, Suspect B.

t_ch: 观景车厢里有多少小圆桌？
t_en: How many small round tables are there in the Parlor Car?

t_ch: 两张小圆桌。真正的星穹列车的成员肯定是知道的。
t_en: 2 small round tables. The real crew member of the Astral Express will certainly know this.

t_ch: 你知道原始博士喜欢吃香蕉吗？
t_en: Do you know Dr. Primitive loves to eat bananas?

t_ch: 我当然知道…吗？你这是什么问题？
t_en: Of course, I know...? What kind of question is that?

t_ch: 你知道上一次我抽卡保底了吗？
t_en: Do you know I hit my hard guarantee the last time I gacha'ed?

t_ch: 你觉得我回答了这个问题能证明什么？
t_en: Even if I can answer that question, what can it prove?

t_ch: 雪衣、寒鸦和藿藿谁最喜欢我？
t_en: Among Xueyi, Hanya, and Huohuo, who likes me the most?

t_ch: 我只知道她们都很喜欢我。
t_en: I only know all three of them like me a lot.

t_ch: 圆周率小数点后一百位是什么？
t_en: What is the number at the <unbreak>100</unbreak>th decimal place for π?

t_ch: 是9…等等，你这恍然大悟的表情是怎么回事？
t_en: It's 9... Wait, did I miss something? Why the surprised look?

t_ch: 好了，我已经心中有数了。
t_en: All right. I already know the answer.

t_ch: 幽府武弁！把嫌疑人甲立刻逮捕！
t_en: Wraith Warden! Arrest Suspect A immediately!
WHAT!?

t_ch: 勘录舍的阴影中蹿出两名幽府武弁，立刻将「无相者」嘟嘟控制了起来。
t_en: Two Wraith Wardens leap out from the shadows of the Domain of Collations and apprehend Shapeless Doot-Doot in an instant.

t_ch: 让您受累了，{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: Sorry that you had to go through all these hassles, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 准确！你到底是怎么判断的？
t_en: Critical hit! How did you know?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，其实我才是假扮的。
t_en: Hahaha, actually, I was the fake.

t_ch: 那我现在把你抓起来？
t_en: It's not too late to order your arrest.

t_ch: 比起你们说话的内容，我更喜欢观察你们在面对意料之外的提问时，展现出的小动作和微表情。嫌疑人甲比你慌乱多了。
t_en: Rather than listening to your conversation, I'm more interested in observing your actions and expressions when facing unexpected questions. It's evident that Suspect A is more anxious than you.

t_ch: 我不服，谛听还没有说话呢，我要听听谛听怎么说！
t_en: I am not convinced! Why hasn't the Diting spoken? I want to hear what the Diting has to say!

t_ch: 我以为这事还需要我来处理，看来已经不需要了…魂停，你做得好。
t_en: I thought I would be required to handle this matter. Seems unnecessary now... Good job, Hunting.

t_ch: 要我说，还有更简单的办法判断你们的真伪。你们俩打一架，不就知道了吗？活下来的那个，才有资格成为{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: In fact, there's an easier method to see which of you is real — wouldn't it be simpler for the two of you to fight? The one who survives will be eligible to be {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 说了半天还是要打啊。
t_en: So I still need to fight after all it's said and done.

t_ch: 那倒不必，你看看{F#她}{M#他}惊慌的样子，根本没有和你交手的本事。
t_en: Not necessary. See how panicked that one is. It does not have the capability to battle.

t_ch: 我舍不得打坏了那张脸…
t_en: I don't want to damage that charming face.

t_ch: 没事的。你看看{F#她}{M#他}惊慌的样子，根本没有和你交手的本事。
t_en: Don't worry. See how panicked that one is? It does not have the capability to battle with you.

t_ch: 「无相者」嘟嘟，你可知罪？还是说，非得用这谛听验一验你的成色？
t_en: Shapeless Doot-Doot, will you admit guilt? Or do we have to get the Diting to verify your identity?

t_ch: 我知道错了……
t_en: I'm guilty...

t_ch: 魂停，你将它带下去吧。
t_en: Hunting, take it away.

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，这次真是给你添麻烦了。请收下这些赔礼，当是这个小小闹剧的补偿吧。此间事毕，寒鸦也先行告退了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, sorry to cause you so much inconvenience. Please accept these gifts as our apology. Now that this case has come to an end, I'll take my leave first.

t_ch: 现在逃犯「无相者」嘟嘟，已经缉拿送入牢房，相信短时间之内它也没法逃脱了。{NICKNAME}大人，请原谅我之前对您的无礼。
t_en: Now that escaped prisoner, Shapeless Doot-Doot, has been sent behind bars, it won't be able to escape again anytime soon. {NICKNAME}, please forgive me for being rude to you before.

t_ch: 没事，其实你做事分寸很好。
t_en: It's fine. It's good that you are doing your job.

t_ch: 谢谢大人理解。
t_en: Thank you for your understanding.

t_ch: 下次要是能擦亮眼睛就好了…
t_en: Keep your eyes peeled next time...

t_ch: 在下一定会将今天发生的一切作为重要的教训。
t_en: I will definitely take what happened today as an important lesson.

t_ch: 实际上在遇到{NICKNAME}来之前，嘟嘟就一直在假扮您，要求关闭掉出入幽囚狱的生物识别系统……
t_en: In fact, even before encountering {NICKNAME}, Doot-Doot had been pretending to be you, fervently insisting that the biometrics recognition system for entering and exiting The Shackling Prison should be deactivated...

t_ch: 现在想来，肯定是在为离开幽囚狱做准备。万幸当时见到你，才间接识破了它的诡计。一旦这家伙跑出去，贻害无穷。十王司在此谢过了。
t_en: Now that I think about it, it seems Doot-Doot was scheming an escape from The Shackling Prison. Thankfully, your timely arrival helped foil its ploy. The thought of Doot-Doot escaping is alarming indeed. On behalf of the Ten-Lords Commission, I extend my gratitude to you.

t_ch: 要是大人还想了解「无相者」嘟嘟，可以到囚室当面了解。放心，以后不会再让它离开囚室了。
t_en: If you wish to know more about Shapeless Doot-Doot, you're welcome to visit its cell. Rest assured, we've taken measures to ensure it won't escape again.

t_ch: 正好，谛听也在路上了，我们开始吧。
t_en: The Diting is already on their way. Let us begin.

t_ch: 寒鸦从一旁路过，听见嫌疑人甲的争吵声便走了过来。
t_en: Hanya, who is passing by, walks over upon hearing the ruckus that the suspect is making.

t_ch: 看看你发现了什么，一本书？幽囚狱里什么样的犯人都有，既然书是精神粮食，也许其中就有某种精神系的犯人，以阅读有营养的书本维生。
t_en: Let's see what you've found... A book? The variety of inmates in this Shackling Prison is truly intriguing. Since books are spiritual food for the soul, maybe there is a spiritual inmate who's an avid reader and relies on them for sustenance.

t_ch: 不过你向来都是将不同世界的不同书籍（甚至不是书籍的东西）悄悄放进背包，不知道什么时候会再次打开它了…嗯，这里请允许我问一下，您真的看吗？
t_en: Then again, you've always been quietly stashing away various books (and even non-books) from different worlds into your bag, never quite sure when you'll look at them again... Hmm, allow me to pose this question, but do you truly intend to read them?

t_ch: 每一本我都看，有意思。
t_en: I read every single one of them. They are interesting.

t_ch: 感谢厚爱，请期待之后的书籍。
t_en: Thank you for your support. Stay tuned for more upcoming books.

t_ch: 标题有意思的，我会看。
t_en: I'll read the ones with interesting titles.

t_ch: 原来如此…这和浏览网络社区是一样的。
t_en: I see... So this is similar to browsing online communities.

t_ch: 早就不看了，看字太累。
t_en: I haven't read them for a long time. Words put me to sleep.

t_ch: 理解理解，如今获取信息的手段太多了。
t_en: I understand. After all, there are many ways of getting information these days.

t_ch: 那么这一本是…《魔阴身考》？
t_en: So, this book is... A Study into the Mara-Struck?

t_ch: 你对这个书名好像有点印象。这本书似乎早在你刚登上仙舟之时，就被列为「列车智库」推荐登记书目了，银河中有一些人正在苦苦寻找这部著作的下落，却没有结果。
t_en: You have a faint recollection of the book's title. It appears to have been listed as a recommended title in the Express data bank when you first embarked on the Xianzhou. Several people in the Cosmos have been desperately searching for its whereabouts to no avail.

t_ch: 将它带上吧，也许负责智库维护的丹恒会感谢你的。
t_en: Take it along with you. Maybe Dan Heng, the caretaker of the data bank, will thank you for it.

t_ch: 拿走。
t_en: Take it.

t_ch: 不感兴趣。
t_en: Not interested.

t_ch: 看来你对魔阴身相关考证兴趣寥寥。毕竟你又不是仙舟人，无须有这方面的担忧…当然你要是有空，可以随时回来取走。
t_en: It appears you're not particularly interested in examinations related to the mara-struck. After all, as someone who's not a Xianzhou native, it's not a concern for you... However, should you find yourself with spare time, feel free to take and read it at your leisure.

t_ch: 因为它可能并不是一本*落在监狱的书*，而是*被关在监狱里的书*。
t_en: Because there's a possibility that this book isn't lost in the prison but it's locked in the prison.

t_ch: 等等，你刚才是不是拿走了那本《魔阴身考》？
t_en: Wait, did you take that book A Study into the Mara-Struck with you just now?

t_ch: 没错，我这人喜欢看书涨知识。
t_en: Yes, I love to read to widen my knowledge.

t_ch: 跟你喜不喜欢没关系，你这是在协助它越狱！
t_en: It's got nothing to do with whether you like to read or not. You are helping it to escape from jail!

t_ch: 你们摆在那里不就是让我拿走…
t_en: Didn't you place it there for me to take...?

t_ch: 让你拿走？不对吧，你这是在协助它越狱！
t_en: For you to take? No way. You are helping it to escape the prison!

t_ch: 我刚负责幽囚狱这个区域巡逻不久，也不太清楚这本书的具体情况。但是依据我的经验来看，它应该是犯了什么事。
t_en: I've only recently been assigned to this section of The Shackling Prison, so I'm uncertain about this book's circumstances. However, judging from my experiences, it's likely here for a reason.

t_ch: 你是认真的吗？不开玩笑？
t_en: Are you serious? Are you sure this isn't a joke?

t_ch: 我就是因为性格认真才被选拔成为十王司狱卒的。在我看来，这本书应该就是犯了错，然后被抓起来了。
t_en: I was hired as a Jailer in the Ten-Lords Commission because my personality is always serious. In my opinion, this book must have done something wrong and been arrested.

t_ch: 找不到这本书的原因找到了…
t_en: So that's the reason no one can find this book...

t_ch: …原来有人一直在找这本书吗？告诉他们，他们没找到的原因，就是这本书被抓起来了，不好意思。
t_en: ...Is anyone searching for this book? Please inform them that this book has been apprehended, which is why it's not readily available. My apologies for any inconvenience.

t_ch: 总之，十王司的判断绝对不会出错，我们绝不会冤枉一本好书的。
t_en: In any circumstance, the Ten-Lords Commission will not make a mistake. We will never mistreat a good book.

t_ch: 但是，罪名到底是什么呢？
t_en: But what exactly is the charge?

t_ch: …没有罪名就不会被收监。你说的有道理，我打算现在快速查询一下。
t_en: ...Anyone without a charge will not be in prison. You're right. Let me do a quick check now.

t_ch: 明白了，我会把书留下来。
t_en: I got it. I will leave this book behind.

t_ch: 很好，我将以…我应该以什么罪名将它重新逮捕呢？我现在立刻查询一下。
t_en: All right. Then I'll... On what charge should I rearrest it? Let me do a quick check.

t_ch: 这位业务稍显不成熟的狱卒在你的面前开始查询起来，一本书到底犯了什么罪，才会被关在幽囚狱中，让银河中那么多人费尽手段也没有找到它？
t_en: The inexperienced and unseasoned prison guard begins her search before you. What grievous offense could a mere book have committed to warrant imprisonment in The Shackling Prison, to the extent that numerous individuals across the Cosmos had gone to great lengths to search for it but had all failed?

t_ch: 奇了怪了，系统上没有任何记录。难道说它并没有犯罪？
t_en: This is weird. There isn't any record in the system. Could it be that it's not a criminal?

t_ch: 这比较符合我的常识…
t_en: That is more in line with my common sense...

t_ch: 你说的有道理，书你可以带走。
t_en: You are right. You may bring the book along with you.

t_ch: 现在我可以带走它了吗？
t_en: So I can take it with me now?

t_ch: 书你可以带走……
t_en: Yes, you may take the book with you...

t_ch: 我还有职务在身，得继续去巡逻了。如果你对《魔阴身考》为何被关在监狱里感兴趣的话，请一定要找司狱赤衣大人问清楚！
t_en: I've got duties to attend to and need to get back on patrol. If you are curious about why the A Study into the Mara-Struck book is locked up in prison, you can seek out Jailer Chiyi for further information.

t_ch: 新来的狱卒挠了挠头离开了这里，这本《魔阴身考》切切实实成为了你的所有物。
t_en: The new prison guard scratched her head and left. A Study into the Mara-Struck is now your book.

t_ch: 你终于拿到了*谁也找不到的《魔阴身考》*，要是有一个世界广播，你应该告诉银河里所有想要找到这本书的朋友。
t_en: You finally get your hands on the unfindable A Study into the Mara-Struck. If only there were a global channel, you would tell everyone in the Cosmos who's trying to find this book.

t_ch: 不过它只是一本普通的书，内容还算有趣却并不值得为它闯入幽囚狱…它仅仅是留下了一道谜题——是谁又出于什么原因，将它带走放进幽囚狱中不见天日？
t_en: But it's just an ordinary book, intriguing in its content but hardly worth breaking into the Shackling Prison for... This only deepens the mystery — who took this book away? And for what reason was it confined within the shadows of the Shackling Prison, hidden from the world's view?

t_ch: 也许找到问题的答案，并不会给你真正的成就感，但如果你有一种谜题未解浑身不适的感觉，就像那位新手狱卒说的那样，去找赤衣大人聊聊吧。
t_en: Perhaps finding the answer to this question will not give you a real sense of accomplishment, but having an unsolved puzzle in your mind makes you uneasy. Just as the new prison guard has suggested, it's time to look for Chiyi.

t_ch: 在监狱里找到了《魔阴身考》…
t_en: I found this book, A Study into the Mara-Struck, in the prison...

t_ch: 这本书有什么特殊之处吗？
t_en: What's so special about this book?

t_ch: 这本书曾被人满世界寻找却无人寻得，以及它被锁在监狱中无人知晓…你将这些经历统统讲给了赤衣，她听完后若有所悟。
t_en: This book had been searched by folks everywhere but no one found it, and it had been concealed in this prison without anyone knowing... You recount the tale to Chiyi, and she listens with comprehension.

t_ch: 若是将这两件事结合起来，不免让人产生了一种「此前大家都找不到这本书，原来是因为它被抓起来了」的感受。
t_en: If I were to take into account these two incidents, it seems to suggest that no one was able to find the book due to its confinement within the prison.

t_ch: 但很显然，这不过是一个凑巧的解释。幽囚狱不会无缘无故收监一本普通的书——我们确实收监某些奇怪又危险的书，绝不会让大人您随意拿走。
t_en: However, it's clear that this is merely a coincidence. The Shackling Prison will not take in an ordinary book for no reason — indeed, we confine only those books of peculiar and perilous nature. They are not to be handled lightly or allowed to be taken away so easily.
